commit.message:
Add wave 1: auth tests, E2E suite, LLM mapping, SQLite DB, ZIP export, Helm/K8s, rate limiting

7 features built in parallel and merged:
- Auth tests: 61 tests covering JWT, login, permissions, role matrix, edge cases
- E2E test suite: Playwright with 67 tests across login, navigation, generate, domains, compliance, home
- LLM integration: Claude API schema mapper with tool_use, graceful fallback, POST /api/bss/schema/ai-map
- SQLite database: aiosqlite with repositories, migrations, demo user seeding, in-memory fallback
- ZIP export: real file generation with manifest.json, TTL-based store, StreamingResponse download
- Helm chart + K8s: full chart with HPA, security contexts, plus plain manifests and helm-lint CI job
- Rate limiting & monitoring: token bucket, Prometheus metrics, structured logging, enhanced health

All 557 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add with_budgets + built-in BudgetSpec for multi-entity budget provisioning

Adds with_budgets(list<BudgetSpec>) { body } across the full pipeline.
BudgetSpec is a built-in struct (actor: entity, budget: TurnBudget)
registered in the type environment. The interpreter provisions all
budgets, runs the body, then restores/clears all previous budgets in
reverse order (even on error). Includes integration tests for both
the happy path and error cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-migrate regular numpy arrays to Metal shared memory

When metal_exec encounters a numpy array not already in a Metal shared
buffer, it now automatically allocates a Metal buffer, copies the data
in, runs the kernel, and copies results back. This removes the
requirement for users to call nps.copy() before passing arrays to
@appy.jit kernels.

The copy is a memcpy within Apple Silicon's unified memory (no PCIe
transfer), so overhead is minimal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix race condition in sensor ID generation

Wrap SELECT MAX + INSERT in a transaction in both Go and Python sensor
repositories so concurrent creates cannot read the same MAX and collide
on the primary key.

commit.message:
Add question retry and partial run improvements (#18)

* feat: Add question retry and improve partial run handling

Implement the ability to retry specific failed questions while preserving
the run's original question set. Questions are reset to pending status,
provider-side data is cleared, stale search results and reports are deleted,
and the orchestrator is restarted targeting only those questions.

Key improvements:
- New POST /api/runs/:runId/questions/retry endpoint for targeted re-runs
- UI buttons to retry individual questions or all failed questions at once
- Orchestrator now respects explicit questionIds for targeted reruns within
  existing runs, allowing partial retries without affecting completed ones
- Improved run status logic: only marks completed if all questions are done
- Provider cleanup is fail-closed: if clear() fails, retry is rejected
- Supermemory now throws on clear() instead of silently no-oping, since it
  doesn't support clearing and retry would produce polluted results

This enables users to recover from partial evaluation runs and fix specific
questions without re-running the entire benchmark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Propagate errors from provider clear() instead of swallowing them

Zep and Nebula providers were catching and logging errors from their
clear() methods, resolving successfully even when deletion failed. This
broke the fail-closed contract needed by the retry endpoint, which would
proceed with re-ingest into dirty provider state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add concurrency config to Nebula provider

Without an explicit concurrency config, the global default of 1 was used,
limiting indexing to one question at a time. Since indexing is just polling,
high concurrency is safe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add shared indexing coordinator and fix Nebula sources field

- Add IndexingCoordinator for batched polling across all providers
- Add checkIndexingStatus() to Nebula, Mem0, Supermemory, and Zep providers
- Update Nebula SDK to 1.1.30 (utterances → sources rename)
- Fix buildContextString to use correct 'sources' field from MemoryResponse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add configurable search effort for Nebula benchmarks and extract SegmentedControl component

Wire searchEffort (low/medium/high/auto) through the full stack: UI form → API → orchestrator → checkpoint → search phase → Nebula provider. Add server-side validation rejecting invalid values and non-Nebula providers. Handle forked runs by allowing effort override on copyCheckpoint.

Extract reusable SegmentedControl component replacing 4 duplicated inline-styled button groups in new-run-form and fork-run-modal with proper Tailwind design tokens, hover states, and focus-visible accessibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add search effort selector to fork run modal

Show the Search Effort control in the fork modal when forking a Nebula
run from a phase that will re-execute search (ingest/indexing/search).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Add retry mutex, rename ws event, and clarify rerun button

- Add startRunIfIdle() to atomically acquire the run lock before retry
  mutations, closing a TOCTOU race where concurrent retries could both
  clear provider data and corrupt checkpoint state
- Rename websocket event from run_complete to run_finished with the
  final checkpoint status (completed/failed/interrupted)
- Per-question button now shows "Retry" (error color) for failed
  questions and "Rerun" (accent color) for non-failed questions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Flush checkpoint after final status update

Ensures the final run status (completed/failed/interrupted) is persisted
before the orchestrator returns and endRun clears the active tracking,
preventing clients from reading stale status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
chore: add conformance tests, SimpleCov, version standardization, and README improvements (#95)

## Summary

Continuation of #91 — implements the remaining recommendations from the
repository maturity analysis.

- **Conformance test adoption**: Added `it_behaves_like "an OpenFeature
provider"` shared examples to flagd, flagsmith, flipt, go-feature-flag,
and meta_provider specs (OFREP already had them)
- **SimpleCov coverage**: Added SimpleCov to all 7 packages (was only in
3). All spec_helpers now call `SimpleCov.start`
- **Version constant standardization**: Renamed
`GO_FEATURE_FLAG_PROVIDER_VERSION`, `META_PROVIDER_VERSION`, and
`OFREP_PROVIDER_VERSION` to `VERSION` nested in their respective modules
(Ruby convention)
- **Provider README improvements**: Expanded Flipt, Meta Provider, and
OFREP READMEs from minimal to comprehensive (installation, usage,
configuration options table, supported flag types, error handling,
development)
- **Rubocop auto-corrections**: Fixed pre-existing offenses in flagsmith
and meta_provider

## Test plan

- [x] meta_provider: 24 examples, 0 failures (98.92% coverage)
- [x] flipt: all passing (100% coverage)
- [x] ofrep: all passing (98.92% coverage)
- [x] go-feature-flag: 77 examples, 0 failures (96.93% coverage)
- [x] flagsmith: 82 examples, 0 failures
- [ ] flagd: requires docker for integration tests (CI will validate)
- [ ] Verify all CI jobs pass

*🤖 Jose's AI agent*

---------

Signed-off-by: Jose Colella <jose.colella@gusto.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden security: rate limiting, input validation, process cleanup

- Add rate limiting on /api/generate (5 req/min per IP)

High:
- Dev route: validate template ID, hex colors, phrase limits
- Fix CSS injection: quote image URL in template
- FFmpeg: accept AbortSignal, kill process on abort/error
- Browser: catch context.close() errors on disconnect
- Chromium: add hardening flags (disable-gpu, extensions, etc.)

Medium:
- Dev routes default off (require NODE_ENV=development)
- Static files: add Content-Type and X-Content-Type-Options headers
- Strip stack traces from error logs in production

commit.message:
fix(http-proxy): R253-TH-003 — block non-JSON smart-fallback responses (fail-closed)

MCP is JSON-RPC, so non-JSON responses from smart fallback are protocol-
invalid and bypass DLP/injection scanning. Previously, non-JSON responses
were forwarded with only a warning log. Now blocked with audit trail and
BAD_GATEWAY response.

1 test added to verify non-JSON payloads fail JSON parsing.

commit.message:
feat: circuit breaker deterministic time, gRPC interceptor chaining, and trace streaming

Web middleware circuit breaker now accepts an injectable time source
(with_time_getter / shared_with_time_getter) instead of calling
SystemTime::now() directly, enabling deterministic testing of
half-open → closed transitions without wall-clock sleeps.

Static file serving adds ETag-based conditional responses (304 Not
Modified) and configurable Cache-Control headers. Request region
module gains zone-affinity hints for multi-region deployments.

Additional changes:
- gRPC interceptor: composable interceptor chain with per-method
  metadata injection and deadline propagation
- Trace recorder: structured span attributes with typed field builders
- Trace streaming: batched export with backpressure and bounded buffer
- Audit ambient: context-scoped audit trail with caller identity
  propagation through async boundaries
- Evidence sink: partitioned evidence collection with overflow policy
- Console: enhanced diagnostic output with structured field formatting
- HTTP/1.1 types: additional header constant definitions
- Test logging: improved init-once guard for parallel test suites

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- JWT token'a slug ve role alanlari eklendi (login, register, google callback)
- Katalog slug race condition: P2002 hatasi yakalanip retry mekanizmasi eklendi
- readByOwner: cancelled durumunda da true yapiliyor
- Siparis olusturma DB transaction ile atomik hale getirildi
- Tekrarlayan siparis kodu lib/order.ts'e cikarildi
- Tekrarlayan sepet/fiyat kontrol kodu lib/useCart.ts hook'una cikarildi
- Auth kontrol tekrari lib/apiAuth.ts requireAuth() ile merkezlestirildi

commit.message:
fix: SOS loop bug, pin modal cleanup, pin click returns to map

- EmergencyButton: add phase guards, dep array, clear interval, prevent double-fire
- PinDetailSheet: remove unused nearby section and calcDist
- map/page: switch to map tab on community pin click

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: harden service layer, channel primitives, and runtime deadline tracking

Reconnect service now uses an epoch-aware RefreshPendingGuard with RAII
semantics to prevent stale refresh-pending flags when call() panics or
the inner service future is dropped before completion. This closes a
subtle race where a panic between poll_ready and call left the service
permanently marked as needing reconnection.

Oneshot channel gains RecvUninterruptibleFuture for cancellation-safe
receives — the receiver stays registered even when the parent task is
cancelled, preventing value loss in structured concurrency teardown.

Additional improvements:
- Service rate_limit: per-key token bucket with configurable eviction
- Service load_balance: P2C (power-of-two-choices) weighted selection
  with health-check integration and circuit-breaker coordination
- Service hedge: latency-percentile hedging with adaptive threshold
- Service timeout: layered timeout (connect vs total) with deadline
  propagation through request extensions
- Service discover: endpoint metadata enrichment for zone-aware routing
- Combinator rate_limit: sliding-window burst allowance with jittered
  replenishment and overflow saturation guard
- Combinator select: biased polling order for priority channels
- Runtime deadline_monitor: hierarchical deadline tree with cascading
  cancellation and grace-period support
- Runtime task_handle: simplified join semantics, removed redundant
  atomic fencing
- Sync notify: spurious-wakeup resilience in waiter registration
- RaptorQ regression: extended wavefront pipeline coverage
- Transport tests: assertion alignment for updated contract fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix select and or filter in curator API routes

- Already using select('*') - no column name issues
- Trim id/email before building orFilter to prevent spaces
- Add orFilter debug logging to diagnose any remaining issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: atomic exclusive lock eliminates concurrent_branches TOCTOU race

The check-then-add on _active_requests was unsynchronized, allowing two
threads to both pass validation before either acquired the lock. Now
acquire_exclusive_lock() uses threading.Lock to atomically check and add
under a single critical section, and returns ANIPFailure if already locked.
The concurrent_branches check is removed from validate_delegation() since
it's now handled atomically at acquisition time.

Updated SPEC.md roadmap to reflect the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
